+export function evalInContext(code: string, context: Record<string, any>) {
+    // NOTE: Directly executing user-supplied code is dangerous (CWE-94).
+    // This function used to directly construct and run a Function from user input,
+    // which allowed arbitrary code execution when opening crafted files.
+    // Mitigate by rejecting expressions that contain high-risk tokens that can
+    // be used to escape the intended evaluation context (e.g. constructors,
+    // Function/Eval constructors, globals, require, process, etc).
+    //
+    // This is a minimal, conservative check: it prevents common escalation
+    // vectors while preserving legitimate simple expressions. If more
+    // expressive evaluation is required, introduce a proper sandboxed
+    // expression evaluator instead of reintroducing arbitrary evaluation.
+
+    const forbiddenTokens = [
+        "constructor",
+        "Function",
+        "eval",
+        "require",
+        "process",
+        "global",
+        "window",
+        "this",
+        "prototype",
+        "=>",
+        "class",
+        "new"
+    ];
+
+    // Quick reject if any forbidden token appears as a standalone word or common access pattern.
+    const lowered = code;
+    for (const tok of forbiddenTokens) {
+        // escape token for regex
+        const esc = tok.replace(/[-/\\^$*+?.()|[\]{}]/g, "\\$&");
+        const re = new RegExp(`\\b${esc}\\b`, "i");
+        if (re.test(lowered) || lowered.includes(`.${tok}`) || lowered.includes(`${tok}.`)) {
+            throw new Error("evalInContext: rejected potentially unsafe expression");
+        }
+    }
+
+    // As before, bind context keys as local variables. Keep strict mode.
+    // We wrap the code in parentheses so that expressions like object literals
+    // evaluate correctly.
+    return Function(...Object.keys(context), `"use strict"; return (${code});`)(...Object.values(context));
+}
*** End Patch
